data.useCase: Classify support tickets by issue type and urgency.
Classify support tickets by issue type and urgency.

message: creating search plan with OpenAI model gpt-5-mini

data:
Create a Hugging Face dataset search plan for post-training a language model.
Domain: customer support
Quality tier: 3
Use case: Classify support tickets by issue type and urgency.
Quality tier definitions:
1 = Fastest: under 10K rows, one tightly matched dataset, optimize for minutes to a few hours.
2 = Fast: 10K-50K rows, 1-2 complementary datasets, moderate filtering.
3 = Balanced: 50K-500K rows, 2-3 datasets, core use case plus edge cases.
4 = Quality: 500K-2M rows, 3-5 diverse high-quali...

analysis.domain_summary: Customer support tickets: short-to-medium text (emails, chat logs, ticket fields) labeled by issue type (billing, technical, account, shipping, etc.) and urgency/severity. Useful datasets should include clear labels or metadata, diverse channels (email/chat/phone transcripts), multiple organizations/verticals to reduce overfitting, and enough volume to cover common and edge-case issue classes. Expect noise, informal language, truncated contexts, threaded messages, and potential PII that must ...
analysis.quality_tier_strategy: Balanced (tier 3): aim to combine 2–3 complementary datasets totaling 50K–500K rows. Prefer one core, well-labeled ticket dataset for issue-type labels plus one dataset with threaded conversations or urgency annotations for edge cases. Apply label mapping to a common taxonomy, class balancing or resampling, and lightweight augmentation (paraphrasing) if gaps remain. Prioritize datasets with clear licenses and documented labeling guidelines; filter and PII-scrub before use.

search_queries: {"search":"customer support tickets","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"downloads","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Find core labeled ticket corpora with issue and/or urgency labels to form the primary dataset."}

{"search":"support ticket dataset","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"likes","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Identify well-liked, curated ticket datasets that may include multi-class labels or structured metadata."}

{"search":"helpdesk logs","task_filter":"conversational","sort":"downloads","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Locate threaded helpdesk/chat logs to capture multi-turn context and urgency cues for edge-case labeling."}

{"search":"customer service emails","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"likes","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Find email corpora with subject/body useful for issue-type classification and extracting urgency signals."}

{"search":"it support tickets","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"trending","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Target vertical-specific tickets (IT) to improve coverage of technical categories and terminology."}

{"search":"support conversations","task_filter":"conversational","sort":"created","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Discover recent conversational datasets that capture modern chat-style support interactions."}

{"search":"incident reports dataset","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"downloads","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Find incident/issue reports that emphasize severity/urgency labels to bootstrap urgency classification."}

{"search":"multi-label support dataset","task_filter":"text-classification","sort":"likes","min_rows":50000,"intent":"Search for datasets annotated with multiple labels (issue type + urgency) to simplify mapping to the target taxonomy."}
ranking_criteria: {"criterion":"Label relevance and granularity","weight":"high","description":"Datasets that include explicit issue-type and/or urgency/severity labels, or rich metadata that maps easily to the target taxonomy, rank highest."}

{"criterion":"Dataset size (usable rows)","weight":"high","description":"Prefer datasets with enough labeled examples to contribute meaningfully toward the 50K+ target; larger single datasets reduce need for heavy augmentation."}

{"criterion":"Label quality and documentation","weight":"high","description":"Clear annotation guidelines, inter-annotator agreement, and label definitions reduce downstream noise and relabeling effort."}

{"criterion":"Domain and channel diversity","weight":"medium","description":"Mix of emails, chat logs, and ticket fields improves generalization; vertical diversity (retail, SaaS, telco) helps coverage of varied issue types."}

{"criterion":"Class balance and coverage of edge cases","weight":"medium","description":"Datasets that cover both common and rare issue types and provide urgency labels or proxies are more valuable, even if smaller."}
{"criterion":"License and PII risk","weight":"high","description":"Prefer permissive/legal-clear datasets; high PII risk requires more preprocessing and can reduce usable rows."}

recommendation_guidance.mixing_strategy: 1) Start with one core, well-labeled ticket dataset for issue-type labels. 2) Add one conversational/helpdesk dataset for multi-turn context and urgency cues. 3) Add a smaller incident/urgency-focused dataset to fill severity labels. Normalize labels to a shared taxonomy, downsample/upsample to address class imbalance, and run PII detection + redaction. Validate labeling quality on a 1–2K hand-checked sample before fine-tuning.
recommendation_guidance.warnings: ["PII/leak risk: support tickets commonly contain personal data—perform mandatory PII detection and removal.","Label mismatch: differing taxonomies across datasets will require mapping and may introduce noisy labels.","License incompatibility: ensure dataset licenses allow your intended use (commercial vs research).","Class imbalance: urgent/severe classes are often underrepresented—plan for augmentation or targeted collection.","Duplicate/near-duplicate records: merging multiple sources can ...

Infer a deterministic normalization recipe for a single Hugging Face dataset so a text-only SFT backend can train on it.
Do not invent columns.
Use the native dataset field names exactly as provided.
Return usable=false when the schema cannot support a meaningful deterministic text or prompt/completion normalization.
Use template_synthesis only when direct column mapping is impossible.
Avoid likely identifier columns such as ticket ids, customer ids, phone numbers, and other obvious PII unles...

message: selecting recommended datasets from 12 candidates with OpenAI model gpt-5-mini

data:
Choose the smallest useful set of already-fetched Hugging Face datasets for the user's post-training use case.
Use only the provided candidates. Do not invent new dataset ids.
Return 1 dataset when a single clear winner is enough.
Return 2-3 datasets only when combining them would materially improve coverage, robustness, or task fit.
Do not return more than 3 datasets.
Prefer domain relevance, task/schema fit, target row-band fit, public usability, license clarity, and overall data usefulness...
